note: Changes that break compatibility with the published 5.x line for documents migrating to v6. Changes that only affect v6's own in-progress spec (the language is still being defined) are deliberately NOT tracked here — this file is strictly the 5 -> 6 migration surface.

changes.title: Bareword names restricted to identifier-like characters
changes.decision: Accepted 2026-06-14: the break is unavoidable and is accepted rather than engineered around. Decided charset: a bareword is [A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]* — letters, digits, and underscore, never starting with a digit. Every other character (',', '(', ')', '.', '+', '&', '#', '@', '$', '^', '*', '!', '?', dash, colon, and a leading digit) requires a quoted-string name. Dash and dot are excluded deliberately: dash collides with the arrow operators and numeric sign, and dot is reserved for member access in the expression language.
changes.summary: Unquoted state / val / property names (the grammar's Atom rule, plus the enum-member rule) are restricted to identifier-like names. 5.x accepted symbol-bearing barewords and leading-digit barewords; v6 requires those to be quoted strings.
changes.breaks: Any 5.x document whose unquoted state / transition / property / enum-member names contain a non-[A-Za-z0-9_] character, or begin with a digit.
changes.migration: Quote the affected names ("in-progress", "node.start", "1") or rename them to identifier form (in_progress).
changes.implementation: Grammar change to AtomFirstLetter / AtomLetter / ValEnumMember plus a corpus + test sweep — tracked in #754, not yet landed.

changes.title: Default jssm import is bare functions, not the Machine class
changes.decision: Decided 2026-06-17 (megaspec §27): the v6 public JS API is bare functions over an opaque machine value, individually importable for tree-shakeability (serves the long-standing #1167 ask). The entire 5.x Machine class is preserved unmodified as an opt-in compatibility layer at the 'jssm/compat' subpath, mechanically delegating to the bare functions (single source of truth; proven by running the 5.x test suite against it verbatim). The default 'jssm' entry resolves to the bare functions.
changes.summary: v6 moves the default 'jssm' import from the Machine class to bare functions (create / transition / can / currentState / addHook / ...), with the machine an opaque data value passed as the first argument. The full 5.x class interface remains available, unchanged, from 'jssm/compat'.
changes.breaks: Any 5.x code using 'new Machine(...)' or class-method calls (m.transition(x)) through the default 'jssm' import.
changes.migration: Zero-edit escape hatch: change the import specifier to 'jssm/compat' (import { Machine } from 'jssm/compat') for verbatim 5.x behavior. Or migrate to the bare-function form: transition(m, x) instead of m.transition(x).
changes.implementation: Extract the state-record type + bare functions from the current class internals; regenerate Machine as a delegating wrapper over them; split entry points + sideEffects:false + a bundle-size test; run the 5.x suite against the compat class as the contract check (megaspec §27, §20 phasing). Not yet landed.
